nodes.standard_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.original_text_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.context: Jihadist group that attempted to recruit Al-Shabaab, later clashed with it.
Splinter group from Al-Shabaab pledging allegiance to ISIL.

edges.evidence_quote: It is allied to the pan-Islamist militant organization al-Qaeda
edges.context: Al-Shabaab is allied with al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Formed in the mid-2000s as a youth militia within the wider military wing of the Islamic Courts Union
edges.context: Al-Shabaab originated as a youth militia within the ICU's military wing.

edges.evidence_quote: Many of its early leaders and members previously belonged not only to the Islamic Courts Union but also to the older al-Ittihad al-Islamiya
edges.context: Many early Al-Shabaab leaders previously belonged to al-Ittihad al-Islamiya.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabaab has clashed with the pro-AMISOM Sufi militias of "Ahlu Sunna Waljama'a".
edges.context: Al-Shabaab has clashed with Ahlu Sunna Waljama'a.

edges.context: Al-Shabaab lists Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin as an ally.

edges.context: Al-Shabaab is alleged to be allied with Houthis, though denied.

edges.evidence_quote: Hizbul Islam, a smaller group of Somali militants, was merged with Al-Shabaab: after suffering military defeats against Al-Shabaab in southern towns, Hizbul Islam was officially absorbed by its former rival in December 2010.
edges.context: Hizbul Islam merged into Al-Shabaab after military defeats.

edges.evidence_quote: on September 24, 2012, Hizbul Islam announced that it would split from Al-Shabaab
edges.context: Hizbul Islam split from Al-Shabaab.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabaab has Kenya-based affiliates, with al-Hijra as its official Kenyan wing from 2012
edges.context: Al-Hijra is the official Kenyan wing of Al-Shabaab.

edges.evidence_quote: then with a unit established in 2013 known as Jaysh Ayman
edges.context: Jaysh Ayman is a Kenya-based affiliate unit of Al-Shabaab.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabab members pledged allegiance to al-Qaeda in 2012 and has reportedly forged ties with al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb , and al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula .
edges.context: Al-Shabaab pledged allegiance to Al-Qaeda in 2012, becoming an official affiliate.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabab members pledged allegiance to al-Qaeda in 2012 and has reportedly forged ties with al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb , and al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula .
edges.context: Al-Shabaab has forged ties with AQIM.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabab members pledged allegiance to al-Qaeda in 2012 and has reportedly forged ties with al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb , and al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula .
edges.context: Al-Shabaab has forged ties with AQAP.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 2012, General Carter Ham of the U.S. Army said that Al-Shabaab, al-Qaeda in Islamic Maghreb (AQIM), and the Nigeria-based Boko Haram were attempting to coordinate their activities, including in funding, training, and explosives.
edges.context: Al-Shabaab attempted to coordinate activities with Boko Haram.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The groups clashed violently on several occasions, most fiercely during 2015, and Al-Shabaab's internal security service continued to arrest, and sometimes execute, suspected ISIS supporters within Al-Shabaab's ranks.
edges.context: Al-Shabaab clashed violently with ISIL after rejecting its overtures.

edges.evidence_quote: In October 2015, senior Al-Shabaab commander Abdul Qadir Mumin and approximately 20 of his followers in Puntland pledged allegiance to ISIL, establishing what became Islamic State in Somalia (ISIS).
edges.context: Islamic State in Somalia is a splinter group from Al-Shabaab.
